latest.title: Full year trading update
latest.content:
14 Jan 2026 07:00
RNS Number : 7785O
Mirriad Advertising PLC
14 January 2026
THIS ANNOUNCEMENT CONTAINS INSIDE INFORMATION FOR THE PURPOSES OF ARTICLE 7 OF THE MARKET ABUSE REGULATION (596/2014/EU) AS IT FORMS PART OF UK DOMESTIC LAW BY VIRTUE OF THE EUROPEAN UNION (WITHDRAWAL) ACT 2018 ("MAR").
14 January 2026
Mirriad Advertising plc
("
Mirriad
" or the "
Company
")
Full year trading update
Mirriad
, a leading provider of virtual product placement ("VPP"), provides an update on trading for the year ended 31 December 2025 and the outlook for 2026.
Subject to completion of the audit, Mirriad's revenue for 2025 is expected to total just over £0.4m (FY24: £1.0m).
Although Q4 sales were disappointing, the Board remains cautiously optimistic about Mirriad's outlook. The Company continues to work closely with its key partners, including Rembrand, and expectations are for a significantly stronger sales performance in 2026. The quieter than expected Q4 followed a broader industry-wide underperformance in media sales during 2025, which affected partners' full suite of products across the sector.
In parallel, the Company has entered discussions with strategic partners in a number of key emerging markets to establish joint ventures. These discussions are aimed at capitalising on Mirriad's significantly reduced campaign delivery costs, enabled by further automation and enhanced scalability. Negotiations are underway at varying stages with potential partners in a number of regions, including India, Indonesia, the Philippines, Thailand and Malaysia, with some being structured around meaningful minimum guarantees. Expansion into the Middle East has progressed well and contributed significantly to revenues in H2.
The Company is also in discussion with a major UK media company to take to market a test campaign and is progressing discussions towards an agreement. In alignment with the indication received from certain partners, the Board anticipates a potentially significant uplift in sales during the upcoming February-March period.
The Company had cash at 31 December 2025 of £1.2 million. This included the £350k R&D tax credit received in December. Costs continue to be carefully managed and, as announced on 19 December 2025, the current cost base is c. £220k per month.
For further information please visit
www.mirriad.com
or contact:
Mirriad Advertising plc
Louis Wakefield, Chief Executive Officer
James Black, Chairman
c/o Allenby
Allenby Capital Limited (Nominated Adviser and Broker
)
James Reeve (Corporate Finance)
Matt Butlin / Lauren Wright (Sales and Corporate Broking)
Tel: +44 (0)20 3328 5656
About Mirriad
The leader in virtual product placement and in-content advertising, Mirriad's multi-patented and award-winning platform can dynamically insert products and brands into Television, SVOD/AVOD, Music, and Influencer content. Mirriad creates net-new revenue opportunities for content owners with an ad format that virtually integrates brands in entertainment content, drives exceptional performance for advertisers and dramatically improves the viewing experience.
Mirriad currently operates in: EMEA, the US (via a Joint Venture with Rembrand), and India.

latest.content:

RNS Number : 2050M
Mirriad Advertising PLC
19 December 2025
THIS ANNOUNCEMENT CONTAINS INSIDE INFORMATION FOR THE PURPOSES OF ARTICLE 7 OF THE MARKET ABUSE REGULATION (596/2014/EU) AS IT FORMS PART OF UK DOMESTIC LAW BY VIRTUE OF THE EUROPEAN UNION (WITHDRAWAL) ACT 2018 ("MAR").
19 December 2025
Mirriad Advertising plc
("
Mirriad
" or the "
Company
")
Trading update
Mirriad, a leading provider of virtual product placement ("
VPP
") provides an update on trading for the year ending 31 December 2025.
Revenue for the second half of the year ("
H2
") is expected to be approximately £200k, with some final revenue still expected to be recognised before the end of December. As a result, full year revenue is expected to be approximately £0.4m. The vast majority of revenue in H2 to date has been derived from ROW outside of the US.
Cash at 30 November 2025 was approximately £1m and the Company anticipates receiving a tax credit of c. £350k shortly. As announced in the interim results, the Company's cost base has been significantly reduced and is currently approximately £220k per month.
Mirriad will provide a full year trading update at the beginning of January.

RNS Number : 3412F
Mirriad Advertising PLC
29 October 2025
29 October 2025
Mirriad Advertising plc
("
Mirriad
" or the "
Company
")
Director dealing
Mirriad Advertising plc (AIM: MIRI), a leading virtual in-content advertising and virtual product placement company, announces that, in order to effect a "Bed and SIPP" transaction, James Black, Chairman of Mirriad, has today sold 8,000,000 ordinary shares in the Company ("
Ordinary Shares
") from his nominee account at a price of 1.0103p per share and then repurchased via his Self-Invested Personal Pension ("
SIPP
") 8,000,000 Ordinary Shares at a price of 1.0105p per share.
As a result of these trades, James Black's total beneficial interest in Mirriad shares remains unchanged at 8,240,000 Ordinary Shares, equivalent to 0.05% of the Company's total issued share capital. Further information is set out in the table below.

company_30s.what_they_do: Mirriad Ltd. builds software that lets advertisers embed ads inside video content, using machine‑learning to match the ad to the viewer’s context. The platform works across streaming services, gaming and social media, turning any visual scene into a brand‑aware moment.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The video‑ad market is growing faster than traditional display ads, and Mirriad’s technology gives brands a way to reach viewers without interrupting the experience. That makes the company a key player in the future of in‑stream advertising, a sector worth billions and still largely untapped.
company_30s.current_state: The business is still early‑stage and has struggled to turn a profit. Cash burn is high, but the company has recently secured a number of new contracts, a JV partnership, and a modest funding round. It is trading at a fraction of its peak, indicating a deep price trough.

rns_narrative: Mirriad has been focusing on stabilising its cash position and proving the business model. A series of RNS over the last year show contract wins, a JV that should open new revenue streams, and a small equity raise that will extend the runway.

Two key themes emerge: (1) **Operational consolidation** – the company is trimming costs, securing repeat business and tightening its product roadmap; (2) **Capital optimisation** – a fresh funding round and tax credit are boosting liquidity and signalling that investors still see upside.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Quiet optimism – the community is waiting for the next big announcement and is willing to hold through the current slump.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: Bullish chatter centres on the potential of the new JV and the fact that a fresh capital injection could let the team focus on product rather than fundraising.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Bears worry that the company’s burn remains unsustainable and that the contract wins are too small to turn the business around quickly.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The tone has shifted from skeptical to cautiously hopeful. Early posts were purely negative, but recent updates have lifted the mood, though the volume remains low.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends for Mirriad is flat and essentially invisible, meaning that any new development could catch the market off‑guard and trigger a sharp price move.

near_term_catalysts: A formal announcement of the JV’s first revenue‑generating project, expected within the next 60 days.
The release of the next quarterly earnings report that shows a significant uptick in revenue or a reduction in burn.
A media partnership that brings a high‑profile brand onto the platform, which would be announced in an RNS or press release.
A spike in trading volume that breaks the recent 50‑day moving‑average support level, indicating renewed buying interest.
expect_next.watch_for: Key signals include a sudden jump in daily volume, a positive earnings surprise, or a clear statement that the company will use the new funding to accelerate product roll‑out. Monitor the 52‑week support zone for a breakout; if price climbs above the upper 20‑day SMA, it would confirm a shift from capitulation to recovery.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock appears to be in the late stage of a deep crash. The APEX score suggests a tight compression around the support area, so a breakout would likely be sharp. A mean‑reversion bounce could start within weeks if the catalysts materialise.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: The company sits well below its historical peak, giving it a large upside if the market recognises the value of its technology. The recent contract wins and JV create a credible path to revenue. Historically, deep crashes in this sector have produced 3‑to‑5× returns once the price recovers to the middle of the 52‑week range.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: If the company fails to turn its burn into revenue, the price could stay stuck or fall further. A lack of new contracts or a failed JV would leave the business in a cash‑tight situation, increasing the risk of a prolonged slump.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential upside: 3× from the current trough to a mid‑range level. Potential downside: the price could decline another 10‑15% if the catalysts fail to materialise, but the drawdown from the peak is already deep, so the overall risk is asymmetric.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter after a confirmed volume spike that breaks the 50‑day SMA, size the position to 10‑15% of your discretionary account, and place a stop 3‑5% below the breakout level. Use a trailing stop at 15% of the peak price to lock in gains as the price climbs.
playbook.if_watching: Hold cash until you see either the JV announcement or a quarterly earnings surprise. If the stock breaks above the 20‑day SMA with volume > 200% of the 20‑day average, consider a small entry to test the move.
playbook.if_passing: None identified – the company is not facing imminent delisting or bankruptcy. The only terminal risk would be a prolonged inability to convert contracts into cash.

ai_insights.bottom_line: Mirriad is a deep‑crashed, tech‑ad firm with a promising JV and fresh funding; a timely catalyst could trigger a sharp mean‑reversion, offering a high‑reward, low‑downside play for contrarian traders.
